items.title: Gemini Now Allows Chat and Personal Information Transfers from Other Chatbots
items.summary: Google's Gemini now enables users to transfer their chat histories and personal data directly from other chatbot platforms, aiming to simplify migration and enhance user experience.

items.title: Wikipedia Cracks Down on AI-Generated Content in Articles
items.summary: Wikipedia has implemented stricter policies to curb the use of AI in writing and editing articles, emphasizing human oversight and factual accuracy for its content.

items.title: Salesforce Launches New Slackbot AI Agent to Compete in Workplace AI
items.summary: Salesforce has introduced an enhanced Slackbot AI agent, intensifying its competition with Microsoft and Google in the rapidly growing market for AI-powered workplace tools.

items.title: Google Translate Transforms Headphones into Live Personal Translators on iOS
items.summary: Google Translate now offers a feature on iOS that turns headphones into real-time personal translators, enhancing communication across language barriers.

items.title: CUA-Suite: A New Dataset for Training Computer-Use Agents with Human Video Demonstrations
items.summary: CUA-Suite introduces a massive, human-annotated video demonstration dataset designed to train AI agents for complex computer-use tasks, pushing the boundaries of autonomous interaction.

items.title: OpenAI Discontinues ChatGPT's Erotic Mode Development
items.summary: OpenAI has reportedly abandoned its experimental 'erotic mode' for ChatGPT, signaling a continued focus on responsible AI development and content moderation.

items.title: Google AI Blog Announces Gemini 3.1 Flash Live for Natural Audio AI
items.summary: Google's AI Blog highlights Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a new development aimed at making audio AI interactions more natural and reliable.

items.title: OpenAI Details Approach to Model Specification for AI Safety and Alignment
items.summary: OpenAI has published insights into its strategy for developing a 'Model Spec,' outlining its commitment to defining and aligning AI behavior for safety and ethical use.

items.title: DA-Flow: Degradation-Aware Optical Flow Estimation Using Diffusion Models
items.summary: Researchers present DA-Flow, a novel method utilizing diffusion models for more accurate optical flow estimation, particularly in the presence of image degradation.

items.title: SIMART: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-Ready Articulated Assets with MLLM
items.summary: SIMART proposes a method to automatically decompose complex 3D meshes into articulated, simulation-ready assets using Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), streamlining virtual environment creation.

items.title: The Future of Coding Emphasizes Communication Beyond Just Code
items.summary: A new perspective suggests that the evolution of coding will increasingly prioritize communication skills over mere code generation, especially with the rise of AI assistants.

items.title: I Tried to Turn Agent Memory Into Plumbing Instead of Philosophy
items.summary: An article explores a practical, engineering-focused approach to managing AI agent memory, moving beyond theoretical discussions to implement robust, scalable solutions.

items.title: Speed vs. Smarts: A Debate on Optimizing Coding Agents
items.summary: This discussion delves into the trade-offs between speed and intelligence in AI coding agents, examining which factor is more crucial for effective software development.

items.title: EVA: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for End-to-End Video Agent
items.summary: EVA introduces an efficient reinforcement learning framework designed for end-to-end video agents, enabling them to learn and perform tasks directly from video inputs.

items.title: T-MAP: Red-Teaming LLM Agents with Trajectory-aware Evolutionary Search
items.summary: T-MAP presents a novel red-teaming framework that uses trajectory-aware evolutionary search to identify vulnerabilities and improve the robustness of LLM agents.
